rolling_30d.summary: Fortress Microgrid's local pages property experienced significant volatility in the last 30 days: sessions dropped 42.3% MoM to 344, though YoY growth remains exceptional at +588.0%, reflecting the property's nascent maturity. Engagement rate declined sharply to 9.0% (down 39.0 pp YoY), indicating that while absolute traffic has surged year-over-year, visitor quality and intent have compressed. Direct traffic dominates at 90.7% of sessions but fell 44.9% MoM, though YoY direct growth is extraordinary at +1542.1%, suggesting seasonal volatility or campaign calendar shifts. High-value conversion events paint a mixed picture: mainsite_clicks (16 events, -30.4% MoM) and calls (1 event, -50% MoM) both declined, while contact_us held flat MoM at 6 events. The homepage (/) remains the traffic engine with 135 sessions (+8.9% MoM), but engagement there is anemic at 3.0%. Geo-targeted Texas pages show promise—/texas generated 60% engagement despite only 5 sessions, suggesting strong product-market fit in specific locations. YTD 2026 performance is robust at 1,898 sessions (+1295.6% vs YTD 2025), though the trajectory is decelerating (Q1 averaged 633 sessions/month; March alone dropped to 354).

quick_wins.title: Leverage high-engagement /texas landing page
quick_wins.detail: The /texas page achieved 60% engagement rate across 5 sessions (vs. 3% on homepage). Replicate its messaging, CTA structure, or targeting strategy across other geo-targeted state pages to lift engagement on /texas/carrollton, /texas/fort-worth, and /texas/levelland variants.

quick_wins.title: Capitalize on mainsite_clicks as funnel accelerator
quick_wins.detail: 16 mainsite_clicks in the period indicate users are progressing to deeper engagement. Test homepage-to-mainsite CTA prominence and positioning to recover the 30.4% MoM decline and reactivate the funnel.

quick_wins.title: Optimize homepage engagement funnel
quick_wins.detail: The homepage captured 135 sessions (39% of total traffic) but only 3 engaged—a 3% rate. Even a modest lift to 6% engagement would convert 3–4 additional engaged sessions monthly; test hero CTA clarity, value prop positioning, or form friction reduction.

potential_warnings.title: Engagement rate collapse YoY (-39.0 pp)
potential_warnings.detail: While sessions grew 588% YoY, engaged sessions only grew 29.2%, indicating traffic quality or targeting has deteriorated significantly. The 9.0% engagement rate suggests most visitors are not finding relevant content or converting intent.

potential_warnings.title: Steep MoM traffic decline (-42.3% sessions, -42.6% engaged sessions)
potential_warnings.detail: Last month underperformed the prior 30-day window substantially. Direct traffic fell 44.9% MoM, and high-value conversions (mainsite_clicks, calls, contact_us) all declined or flatlined, indicating potential seasonal drop-off, budget reduction, or campaign pause.

potential_warnings.title: Phone calls bottoming out (1 event, -50% MoM)
potential_warnings.detail: With only 1 call conversion in 30 days, the sales funnel is critically thin. This may reflect low-intent traffic or friction in the call-to-action path and warrants immediate review of click-to-call implementation and page messaging.

potential_warnings.title: Decelerating monthly growth trajectory
potential_warnings.detail: Q1 2026 averaged 633 sessions/month, but March dropped to 354 (down ~37% from February). If this trend continues, April–June may see further contraction despite strong YoY comps.

biggest_opportunities.title: Revitalize direct traffic quality and intent
biggest_opportunities.detail: Direct traffic represents 90.7% of sessions but engagement is minimal. Implement UTM tagging or enhance on-site messaging to clarify value proposition for direct visitors who may arrive without context; test dedicated landing pages for key service offerings (microgrids, solar, storage) to segment intent.

biggest_opportunities.title: Scale geo-targeted Texas pages with proven messaging
biggest_opportunities.detail: Five regional pages (/texas/carrollton, /texas/fort-worth, /texas/levelland, /texas/wichita-falls) each generated 3 sessions with 33% engagement. Allocate PPC or organic budget to scale these high-intent pages and replicate their CTA structure across lower-performing geo variants.

biggest_opportunities.title: Rebuild contact_us and booking funnel
biggest_opportunities.detail: contact_us (6 events) and booking events are critically low; no schedule_/appointment_ events are logged. Implement clear, above-the-fold contact/booking CTAs on /texas landing pages and homepage to capture intent before visitors exit; A/B test form field reduction to lower friction.

biggest_opportunities.title: Diagnose and recover organic search opportunity
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic search represents only 7.3% of traffic (25 sessions) with no YoY growth (-3.8% YoY). This suggests SEO underinvestment or ranking degradation; audit keyword rankings for high-intent terms (e.g., 'microgrid Texas,' 'solar storage Carrollton') and prioritize content/backlink strategy to capture non-branded, high-intent search demand.

last_calendar_month.summary: March 2026 saw a significant MoM contraction (-37.5% sessions, -36.0% engaged sessions) after strong January and February performance, though YoY growth remains exceptional (+637.5% sessions, +39.1% engaged sessions). Direct traffic dominates at 91.5% of sessions but declined -39.7% MoM, suggesting seasonal or campaign-driven volatility rather than organic search strength. High-value conversion events show mixed signals: mainsite_clicks grew +20.0% YoY (18 events) but declined -10.0% MoM, while calls dropped -50.0% MoM to just 1 event. The homepage (/) drove 137 sessions but engaged only 2.2%, while /texas pages demonstrated substantially higher engagement rates (57.1% on /texas itself). YTD 2026 has captured 1,898 sessions versus 136 in 2025, representing a +1,295.6% surge, though the deceleration from 978→566→354 monthly sessions suggests need for sustained acquisition strategy to maintain momentum.

quick_wins.title: Leverage /texas landing page performance
quick_wins.detail: /texas achieved 57.1% engagement rate (4 of 7 sessions engaged) and grew +250.0% YoY, significantly outperforming the homepage's 2.2% engagement rate. Replicate messaging and structure from /texas across other regional pages.

quick_wins.title: Expand high-intent location-specific pages
quick_wins.detail: Multi-location pages (/texas/carrollton, /texas/fort-worth, /texas/levelland, /texas/wichita-falls) each drove 33.3% engagement on minimal traffic. Scaling these pages with paid or organic promotion could yield disproportionate conversion gains.

quick_wins.title: Maintain mainsite_clicks momentum
quick_wins.detail: mainsite_clicks grew +20.0% YoY to 18 events (high-value conversion), despite -10.0% MoM decline. This channel demonstrates funnel depth and should remain a tracking priority for attribution.

potential_warnings.title: Steep MoM traffic decline signals campaign or seasonality reset
potential_warnings.detail: Sessions dropped -37.5% from February to March (566→354), following even sharper drops would indicate a structural problem rather than typical variance. Verify whether January–February benefited from time-limited campaigns that ended.

potential_warnings.title: Call conversions have collapsed
potential_warnings.detail: Calls fell -50.0% MoM (2→1 event) and appear to be a new tracking issue or genuine funnel leakage; only 1 call in March represents critical underperformance for a service-based business. Audit call tracking implementation and review phone number visibility on high-traffic pages.

potential_warnings.title: contact_us event declining across both timeframes
potential_warnings.detail: contact_us dropped -16.7% MoM and -37.5% YoY (down to 5 events), suggesting either a form UX issue or reduced visitor intent. This should be cross-checked with CRM or email submission data to confirm true conversion loss.

potential_warnings.title: Homepage engagement rate severely lags expectations
potential_warnings.detail: The homepage (/) captured 137 sessions (38.7% of traffic) but engaged only 3 (2.2% engagement rate), acting as a traffic sink rather than funnel driver. Poor homepage-to-interior conversion may explain low overall engagement at 9.0%.

potential_warnings.title: Organic search remains negligible and flat
potential_warnings.detail: Organic Search delivered only 24 sessions (6.8% of total) with 0.0% YoY growth, indicating weak SEO visibility or competitive disadvantage. Over-reliance on Direct traffic (91.5%) creates vulnerability to campaign fluctuations.

biggest_opportunities.title: Rebuild and stabilize Direct traffic acquisition
biggest_opportunities.detail: Direct traffic plummeted -39.7% MoM despite +1605.3% YoY growth, likely reflecting campaign spend or referral source volatility. Implement a disciplined retargeting and email nurture strategy to create recurring direct sessions independent of external campaigns.

biggest_opportunities.title: Scale organic search presence systematically
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic Search represents only 6.8% of sessions (24 events) with flat YoY growth; Texas-specific service pages (/texas/carrollton, /texas/fort-worth) present high-intent keywords with minimal competition. Target 'Fortress Microgrid near me' and location-based terms with local SEO and content expansion.

biggest_opportunities.title: Diagnose and fix homepage-to-interior conversion funnel
biggest_opportunities.detail: 137 homepage sessions with only 2.2% engagement suggests a critical messaging, design, or CTA misalignment. Conduct user testing and A/B test homepage CTAs to drive visitors toward /texas regional pages or high-intent service pages rather than internal navigation.

biggest_opportunities.title: Prioritize call tracking and phone visibility audit
biggest_opportunities.detail: With only 1 call recorded in March, verify that phone numbers are prominently displayed, click-to-call is enabled on mobile, and tracking parameters are correctly fired. This is likely a quick win with outsized impact on high-value conversions.
